lang.label: Language
login.title: MarlinSpike — Login
common.name: Name
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.workspace_title_rest: keeps capture intake, live execution, and assessment outputs in one operating view.
dashboard.workspace_subtitle: Use this page as the working surface for project-scoped scanning, recent output review, and active-run follow-through. The goal is fast orientation first, then smooth movement into topology and report analysis.
dashboard.kpi_capture_inventory_detail: PCAPs ready in the selected project.
dashboard.kpi_report_outputs_detail: Recent assessment artifacts available for review.
dashboard.scan_workspace_subtitle: Choose capture source, execution mode, and project scope before launching a run.
dashboard.command_hint: Full Chain runs all stages (ingest, dissect, topology, risk). Individual stages can be re-run separately to refine results without re-ingesting the PCAP.
dashboard.profile_hint: Fast skips deep packet inspection for quicker results. Full enables all DPI stages including protocol-level field extraction, OT command analysis, and malware IOC scanning.
dashboard.bpf_hint: Berkeley Packet Filter expression applied during ingestion. Use to focus analysis on specific hosts, subnets, or protocols.
dashboard.skip_ephemeral_hint: Excludes connections on ports 49152+ from the topology map. These are typically OS-assigned client ports that add noise. Disable for C2 beaconing or data exfiltration analysis where high ports may be significant.
dashboard.upload_drop_hint: Drop PCAP file here or click to browse
dashboard.upload_hint: Drop a .pcap, .pcapng, or .cap file here, or click to browse.
dashboard.recent_reports_subtitle: Open, baseline-compare, or remove the latest generated assessment outputs for the active project.
dashboard.recent_scans_subtitle: Track recent execution state, re-attach to live work, and retry failed file-based runs.
dashboard.workspace_files_empty: No PCAPs loaded for the selected project yet.
dashboard.workspace_files_ready: PCAPs ready for intake in the active project.
dashboard.workspace_reports_detail: Latest output updated {when}. Open the viewer when you are ready to work it.
dashboard.workspace_reports_empty: No reports generated for the selected project yet.
dashboard.workspace_scans_empty: No recent scan activity loaded yet.
dashboard.run_summary_one: 1 live run currently attached for follow-through.
dashboard.run_summary_many: {n} live runs currently attached for follow-through.
dashboard.select_sample_required: Select a sample PCAP from the library
dashboard.scans_empty_sub: Start a scan from the workspace above.
reports.title: Reports
reports.compare_hint: Select two reports using the checkboxes to compare them as a Baseline Drift analysis — identifies new, removed, and changed assets between captures.
reports.empty_sub: Upload a PCAP and run a scan to generate your first report.
reports.empty_run_a_scan: Run a scan to generate your first report.
reports.compare_limit: Select only 2 reports for baseline and drift review
scans.title: Scans
scans.empty_sub: Start your first scan from the Dashboard.
scans.retry_started: Scan retried — check Dashboard for progress
projects.title: Projects
projects.empty_sub: Projects let you group reports captured at the same site or time window.
projects.overview_empty: No reports in this project yet.
projects.workspace_title_rest: keeps capture intake, scan follow-through, and assessment outputs attached to the project that owns them.
projects.workspace_subtitle: Use this surface to move from PCAP intake to report review without losing project context. The project becomes the unit of work, and the workspace shows what is ready, what is running, and what to open next.
projects.kpi_pcap_inventory_empty: No capture files loaded for this project yet.
projects.kpi_pcap_inventory_ready: PCAPs available for scanning in this project.
projects.kpi_project_activity_empty: No scan history for this project yet.
projects.kpi_open_next_detail: Upload a PCAP, select a file, and start a scan.
projects.no_reports_sub: Upload a PCAP and run a scan — the project overview rolls up assets, findings, protocols, and ATT&CK coverage across every report in this project.
projects.asset_filter_placeholder: Filter by MAC, IP, role, vendor…
projects.upload_drop_hint: Drop PCAP file here or click to browse
projects.empty_files_select: Select a project to view files.
projects.empty_reports_select: Select a project to view reports.
projects.empty_history_select: Select a project to view history.
projects.no_selection_title: Select a project from the sidebar
projects.no_selection_sub: or create a new one to get started.
projects.confirm_delete: Delete project "{name}" and all its files? This cannot be undone.
projects.compare_limit: Select only 2 reports for baseline and drift review
projects.kpi_pcap_ready: Capture files ready for intake and scan execution.
projects.next_watch_history_detail: A run is active for this project. Stay on the History tab for execution updates.
projects.next_run_selected_detail: Capture intake is ready. Choose a file and launch a project-scoped scan.
projects.empty_pcap_sub: Upload a capture file above to get started.
projects.empty_history_sub: Start a scan from the Files tab.
projects.upload_trim_notice: File trimmed to first {trimmed} for processing (original: {original}). Full version archived.
projects.severity_empty_legend: Empty severity profile — nothing to triage.
projects.assets_truncate: Showing first 200 of {total} — refine the filter to narrow further.
projects.protocol_tooltip: {name} — seen in {seen} of {total} reports ({packets} packets total)
profile.title: Profile
users.title: Users
audit.title: Audit Log
system.title: System
findings.title: Capabilities Catalog
findings.intro: This page is a source-backed catalog of what MarlinSpike can currently surface across core report findings, Stage 2 DPI coverage, Stage 4b malware matching, and ATT&CK report mappings. It is a product capability view, not a list of what was detected in any one environment.
findings.catalog_total_caption: {total} total after combining report, DPI, malware, and ATT&CK sources.
findings.search_placeholder: Search titles, ids, fields, families, rationale
findings.source_tip: Source filters by where the capability originates: engine (core analysis chain), mitre (ATT&CK rule packs), malware (IOC matching), or dpi (deep packet inspection).
findings.type_tip: Type distinguishes capability kinds: classification (finding-driven technique inferences), coverage (platform-level passive recon), detection (malware and IOC rules), field (protocol field extraction).
findings.family_tip: Family groups related capabilities by functional area: e.g. Passive Recon, Controller Manipulation, Zone Traversal, Data Exposure, Command and Control.
findings.empty_state: No coverage entries matched the current filters.
system.drop_zone: Drop PCAP files here to upload, or click to select
live.title: Live
live.waiting_subtitle: Nodes will appear as MarlinSpike discovers devices
assets.title: Asset Inventory
diff.title: Baseline Drift
viewer.title: MarlinSpike — {filename}
viewer.subtitle: Breach triage, topology, and portable report review from a single report artifact.
viewer.kpi.critical_high_detail: Risk records linked to the current report artifact
viewer.kpi.auth_gaps_detail: Assets where authentication was not observed in traffic
viewer.kpi.priority_targets_detail: High-value assets surfaced for immediate responder review
viewer.rail.dashboard_note: Operator snapshot, queues, and first-pass triage.
viewer.rail.map_note: Topology view, path review, and live node selection.
viewer.rail.traffic_note: Top talkers, conversation ledger, and protocol byte distribution.
viewer.rail.findings_note: Responder findings, suspicious channels, and action queues.
viewer.rail.protocols_note: Per-dissector drilldown — Modbus, S7, DNP3, CIP, IEC104, MMS, GOOSE, BACnet, DNS.
viewer.rail.evidence_note: DPI-enriched sessions, identities, hunt pivots, and module views.
viewer.rail.assets_note: Asset ledger, identity depth, and evidence-heavy hosts.
viewer.rail.intel_note: Protocol coverage, ATT&CK mapping, and extension intelligence.
viewer.rail.risk_note: High-severity findings, Purdue violations, and writable exposure.
viewer.rail.reports_note: Portable artifact review, exports, and extension supplements.
viewer.utilities.label: Utilities
viewer.sidebar.no_asset_help: With JavaScript enabled you can click a node to inspect it in place. Without JavaScript, use the rendered queues below to jump directly into asset sections in the report.
viewer.sidebar.priority_empty: No attack-prioritized assets were surfaced in this report.
viewer.sidebar.auth_gaps_empty: No auth-gap assets were identified in this report.
viewer.sidebar.write_paths_empty: No write-capable or programming-capable paths were identified.
viewer.sidebar.external_empty: No C2 or external-communication indicators were produced for this report.
viewer.dashboard.rail_hint: Use the rail to pivot between topology, risk, evidence, and extension intelligence without losing report context.
viewer.traffic.empty: No conversation data was preserved in this report.
viewer.traffic.anom.empty: No beaconing, high-entropy DNS, or unsecured OPC sessions flagged in conversations.
viewer.proto.empty_no_conversations: No conversation data was preserved in this report.
viewer.proto.roster_summary: {active} of {total} dissector families produced evidence in this capture. Silent families are listed for completeness so the absence of a protocol is itself part of the report.
viewer.proto.modbus.label: Modbus / Modbus-TCP
viewer.proto.s7.label: Siemens S7
viewer.proto.dnp3.label: DNP3
viewer.proto.iec104.label: IEC 60870-5-104
viewer.proto.cip.label: CIP / EtherNet-IP
viewer.proto.mms.label: MMS (IEC 61850)
viewer.proto.goose.label: GOOSE (IEC 61850)
viewer.proto.bacnet.label: BACnet
viewer.proto.omron.label: OMRON / FINS
viewer.proto.profinet.label: PROFINET
viewer.proto.opc.label: OPC
viewer.proto.dns.label: DNS
viewer.legend.title: Map Legend
viewer.report.dpi_empty: No enriched DPI observables were preserved in this report.
viewer.report.mitre.inferred_blurb: Techniques inferred from passive findings and external communication indicators.
viewer.report.mitre.no_techniques: No ATT&CK techniques were inferred from the observed findings.
viewer.report.mitre.unmapped_categories: {count} finding categories remain unmapped in the active ATT&CK pack.
viewer.provenance.unique_macs_title: Distinct MAC addresses observed in capture
viewer.toolbar.status.hint: Use / to jump to search and Esc to clear.
viewer.sel.title: Selected Asset
viewer.sel.empty_body: Select a node, queue item, or finding to pull its classification, services, paths, and protocol evidence into view.
viewer.sidebar.queue_empty: No assets match the current focus.
viewer.sidebar.findings_empty: No risk findings were produced for this report.
viewer.finding.rem.NO_AUTH_OBSERVED: Implement authentication for all OT protocols where supported
viewer.finding.rem.ICS_EXTERNAL_COMMS: Isolate ICS network from internet. Implement DMZ architecture with data diodes or proxies for any required external data flows. Investigate why OT assets are reaching public addresses — potential data exfiltration, misconfigured NAT, or unauthorized connectivity.
viewer.finding.rem.EXTERNAL_IPS_OBSERVED: Verify expected external communications. ICS networks should minimize internet exposure.
viewer.finding.rem.CROSS_PURDUE: Implement zone segmentation with firewall rules enforcing Purdue model boundaries
viewer.finding.rem.CLEARTEXT_ENG: Enable TLS for S7comm, use VPN for engineering access, implement MFA
viewer.finding.rem.MODBUS_WRITE_ANON: Restrict write access to authorized engineering workstations only
viewer.finding.rem.S7_PROGRAM_ACCESS: Disable remote program access, require physical key switch for programming mode
viewer.finding.desc.NO_AUTH_OBSERVED: {count} device(s) using auth-capable protocols show no authentication exchanges
viewer.finding.desc.ICS_EXTERNAL_COMMS: OT assets are communicating with public internet addresses ({ips}). ICS assets should not have direct internet connectivity.
viewer.finding.desc.EXTERNAL_IPS_OBSERVE: Public internet addresses observed in capture: {ips}
viewer.finding.desc.CROSS_PURDUE: Cross-Purdue-level communications detected — supervisory layer bypassed
viewer.finding.desc.CLEARTEXT_ENG: Engineering operations conducted over cleartext protocols
viewer.finding.desc.S7_PROGRAM_ACCESS: S7comm program upload/download detected — PLC logic exposed
viewer.timeline.title: Capture timeline
viewer.traffic.extract_title: Extract sub-PCAP for this conversation (honours active time window)
viewer.asset_ctx.title: Asset context
viewer.asset_ctx.notes_ph: Free text — change history, hand-off context, anything site-specific.
viewer.asset_ctx.delete_confirm: Clear this asset's context? This removes the tag from the project.
viewer.note.bad_status: Invalid status. Allowed: open, investigating, resolved, false_positive.
iocs.title: Threat-hunting IOCs
iocs.no_lists: No IOC lists yet for this project. Create one below.
iocs.pick_list_hint: Pick a list on the left, or create a new one.
iocs.bulk_import_hint: Paste IOCs — one per line. Auto-detects IPv4/v6, MAC, OUI, sha256, md5, domain.
iocs.bulk_paste_ph:
# Comments and blank lines are ignored
21.2.2.2
00:1c:06:27:64:11
c2.example.com
iocs.entries_truncated: Showing first 200 of {count} entries — open the API for the full set.
iocs.no_hits: No hits across the project's reports.
iocs.scan_truncated: Output truncated at 1000 hits — refine the list or scan a smaller scope.
iocs.scanning: Scanning every report in the project…
iocs.delete_confirm: Delete this list and all its entries?
